content.full_text:
July 27, 1976

p 785-072243

Thomas E. Rankin
Attorney at Law
1661 Capistrano Avenue
Berkeley, California 94707

Dear Mr. Rankin:

Thank you for your letter of July 22, 1976 requesting
campaign disclosure information applicable to labor organiza-
tions participating in county and special district election
campaigns.

The Political Reform Act does not contain specific pro-
visions for labor organizations; rather they are subject to
the reporting requirements of all political committees. En-
closed are informational materials describing the formation of
political committees and their filing obligations.

Ordinarily labor organizations qualify as "recipient
committees" by collecting $500 or more in a calendar year in
a political fund or through earmarked dues.

If you have questions after reviewing the enclosures,
please contact me.

Sincerely,

Han Hamilton
Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

Enclosures

bcc: Jeanne P.

July 27, 1976

p 78-07-043

Thomas E. Rankin
Attorney at Law
1661 Canistrano Avenue
Berkeley, California 94707

Dear Mr. Rankin:

Thank you for your letter of July 22, 1976 requesting
campaign disclosure information applicable to labor organiza-
tions participating in county and special district election
campaigns.

The Political Reform Act does not contain specific pro-
visions for labor organizations; rather they are subject to
the reporting requirements of all political committees. En-
closed are informational materials describing the formation of
political committees and their filing obligations.

Ordinarily labor organizations qualify as "recipient
committees" by collecting $500 or more in a calendar year in
a political fund or through earmarked dues.

If you have questions after reviewing the enclosures,
please contact me.

Sincerely,

Nan Hambleton
Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

Enclosures

bcc: Jeanne P.

THOMAS E. RANKIN
ATTORNEY AT LAW
1661 CAPISTRANO AVENUE
BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94707
(415) 525-7126

July 22, 1976


Michael Bennett, Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
925 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento ,Ca. 95814

Dear Mr. Bennett:

I would appreciate receiving a copy of any of your rules and
regulations which apply to a labor organization's
participation in county and special district election
campaigns.

Thank you very much.


Sincerely yours,

Thomas E. Rankin

sections.question_synthetic: What campaign disclosure requirements and rules apply to labor organizations participating in county and special district election campaigns?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Labor organizations are not subject to specific Political Reform Act provisions but are instead treated as political committees subject to standard reporting requirements. They ordinarily qualify as 'recipient committees' when collecting $500 or more annually in a political fund or through earmarked dues.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document appears duplicated in source text. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers, but request and response content are clearly identifiable. Document dated July 27, 1976 (response) to incoming July 22, 1976 request. Response signed by Nan Hambleton, Special Compliance Representative.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What campaign disclosure requirements and rules apply to labor organizations participating in county and special district election campaigns?

CONCLUSION: Labor organizations are not subject to specific Political Reform Act provisions but are instead treated as political committees subject to standard reporting requirements. They ordinarily qualify as 'recipient committees' when collecting $500 or more annually in a political fund or through earmarked dues.

The Political Reform Act does not contain specific provisions for labor organizations; rather they are subject to the reporting requirements of all political committees. If you have questions after reviewing the enclosures, please contact me. Sincerely, Nan Hambleton Special Compliance Representative NH:bw Enclosures bcc: Jeanne P. This is a letter dated July 22, 1976, from Thomas E. Rankin, an attorney at law, to Michael Bennett, Executive Director of the Fair Political Practices Commission in Sacramento, California. The letter requests a copy of any rules and regulations that apply to labor organizations' participation in county and special district election campaigns. The letter begins with: "Michael Bennett, Executive Director Fair Political Practices Commission 925 J Street, Suite 522 Sacramento, Ca. 95814 Dear Mr. Bennett: I would appreciate receiving a copy of any of your rules and regulations which apply to a labor organization's participation in county and special district election campaigns. Thank you very much. Sincerely yours, Thomas E. Rankin" Thomas E. Rankin signed the letter at the bottom.
embedding.summary: FPPC response to an attorney requesting information about labor organization participation in county and special district elections, clarifying that labor organizations are subject to standard political committee reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act.
